behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real `git` rename detection/output formatting from a subprocess (REAL-INFRA, SYSTEM TRIAD happy-path contract). A mocked diff/status payload can hide failures in parsing real rename records. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: it is about git diff categorization only, with no AI/ML decision-making or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against that repo via subprocess, and assert stdout report shows exactly one rename and no corresponding add/delete for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary-diff handling commonly fails at real subprocess/encoding boundaries (bytes, locale, NULs), which mocks often hide (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no model output quality or chained agentic steps are involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify a binary file, invoke the CLI as an actual process, assert zero crash/non-error exit, and assert binary file is included in the reported counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly exercises a real git error-path/edge-state (`HEAD` absent) and subprocess exit/error semantics (SYSTEM TRIAD error-path contract). Mocking git responses can miss real behavior differences. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no AI/ML dependency and no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with zero commits, run the CLI process, assert successful handling (no crash) and an empty structured report on stdout.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: The behavior requires proving externally visible correctness against an actual repository history (round-trip from real repo state to CLI stdout report), not just internal logic. Real git subprocess behavior and history interpretation are part of correctness. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no LLM/model judgment and no chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run the CLI, and assert reported counts exactly match the known ground truth derived from that real history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged relies on real git index/worktree state and subprocess behavior; in-process fakes can miss index semantics and command differences (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: this is git state handling only, not AI/ML output or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent staged and unstaged change scenarios, run the CLI for each scenario as a process, and assert the structured stdout outputs are identical.
